gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 initially found that the legacy facade could recurse through document public wrappers and then that private helper aliases ignored legacy monkeypatch hooks. I removed document-global mutation, gave the legacy facade local wrappers/helpers, added no-recursion/no-leak/private-hook regressions, and final re-review approved with no blocking findings.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_storage_benchmark.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py tests/test_release_evidence.py tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_benchmark_plan.py
repository secret-pattern scan across docs, source, tests, databricks, pr-evidence, and GitHub metadata
GPT-5.5 follow-up review after compatibility fixes
what_changed: Moved the storage benchmark implementation into document_kv_cache.storage_benchmark with a curated public star-import surface.
Reworked restaurant_kv_serving.storage_benchmark into a legacy compatibility facade that preserves broad legacy imports and legacy namespace monkeypatch hooks.
Added regression coverage for legacy CLI hooks, serializer recursion, document namespace isolation, private helper monkeypatches, public package exports, and governance allowlists.
why: Storage benchmark ownership should live in the open-source document package while existing restaurant namespace users continue to work during the migration.
